item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe do an edit on the text to remove references to images, now that they've been removed due to usage regulations?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noting that the images are from here, with no indication that they have been released under a license compliant with the wiki's (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, they have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The main anomaly isn't interesting, or have much material beyond "look at these cool pics", but the interview is delightfully unsettling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm fascinated by the interview log, and I'm glad the interviewee's requests were mainly met, it adds a sense of realism to the conversation.

Also, I'm glad this isn't an AWCY? article. The mysterious bearded men are incredibly interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My thoughts:
Advantaged is An Amenti then Always Artless in Assessment
All ideas heard are perceived as perfect ideas regardless of subjects previous views or any logical

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Edits Made. Timer Stopped.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ show block
– hide block

When I was reading this article, I was immediately reminded of SCP-1528, which alters the perception of people in a similar way to this eyeball. So, I thought it could be a fitting crosslink.

I propose the following addition at the end of the description, and before the addendum.

Due to similar anomalous properties between SCP-1497 and SCP-1528, investigation into possible connections is currently under consideration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …What GOI? Are We Cool Yet? got removed from this literally years ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this would have been more effective if you explored its active effect more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The entire time I was reading it, I was waiting for the AWCY? payoff and was kind of disappointed that it never came. Even without their blatant signature, this still feels exactly like one of their pieces.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, I thought making it a AWCY? skip made more sense. Maybe that's just me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also upvoted for this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Better Off Blind Than Beguiled by Beauty."

"Beguiled by Beauty" refers to the actual effects. Them being blind has nothing to do with the anomaly, those responsible just kidnapped some blind people to say it's better to not see anything at all than see through heavily rose-tinted glasses.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Recovery incident does not explain why they were (still are?) blind. It is not in description of object. If blindness is caused by prolonged exposure, this should be noted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it in draft, like it now. Very intrigued by the other objects - I can maybe imagine that the tooth makes you appreciate the taste of everything, but the brain and the heart… nope

item.title: Re: Tweaks
item.content:encoded: Removing that line in the photo caption was maybe the best change. +1 from me.

item.title: Tweaks
item.content:encoded: Time to invalidate most of the previous comments! After getting some more feedback, I've decided to revoke the AWCY? status of 1497. It was somewhat glued on and it didn't really contribute to the article. The changes are only the last line in the note and removing "coming soon to a gallery near you!" on the photo caption.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: AWCY seems to be regaining popularity this week for some reason :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does the effect end once there's a new -1? I also wonder what level of cognitive dissonance would result from a -1 exposed to a clear auditory threat, and then allowed to see the source.

But I don't know if I like this, either. I'm actually feeling like AWCY is tacked on here. I'm not sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Almost from the start I went "AWCY? or MCaD".
Good on you for having the vibe down. Totally see the reason they'd of made this. …No pun intended. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, with a name like TwistedGears I would have thought you'd be into CotBG…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first one was SCP-1672.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Out of curiousity, what's your first SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's ok… But something seems off about the AWCY logic here. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hah! I liked the bit where they appreciated the aesthetic qualities of knives being pointed at them by angry violent people. That was my upvote moment. Good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Second one! Another AWCY? skip. Big thanks to Randomini, UglyFlower, tahunu, and everybody else in chat who helped out!
